description: This figure presents an oxide map for the Fe-Mn-Al ternary alloy system at 800°C, constructed as a compositional diagram with manganese (0–40 wt.%) on the horizontal axis and aluminium (0–15 wt.%) on the vertical axis. The map delineates four distinct regions (I–IV) characterized by different oxidation scale morphologies: Region I (low Al, <6%) shows alumina scales interspersed with large iron-rich oxide nodules; Region II (duplex α+γ phase field) exhibits alumina with bulky manganese-rich oxides and wormlike oxide threads; Region III (fully ferritic α, approximately 5–10% Mn and 6–10% Al) develops continuous protective alumina scales with minimal weight gain; and Region IV (high Al, >9%) displays alumina scales penetrated by small manganese-rich nodules prone to spallation. Superimposed phase boundaries indicate the alloy microstructure, marking single-phase ferrite (α), austenite (γ), dual-phase α+γ, and β-Mn regions. The map demonstrates that fully ferritic compositions are essential for protective oxidation, whereas austenite promotes scale breakdown, identifying Fe-(5–10)%Mn-(6–10)%Al as the optimum oxidation-resistant composition range.
